svg.title: Need for IoT Systems Management
svg.desc: Educational diagram explaining why IoT systems management is essential for large-scale deployments

g.data-narration: Introduction to the need for IoT systems management in handling billions of connected devices
IoT connects billions of smart devices that generate and exchange data, requiring proper management systems
g.text: IoT envisions billions of smart devices constantly generating, transmitting, and receiving data.
But connecting devices is not enough, we must monitor, control, and secure this vast network.

g.data-narration: Managing IoT is more complex than traditional IT due to massive scale, heterogeneity, and security challenges
g.text: Managing IoT is fundamentally more complex than traditional IT networks due to:
Massive scale (thousands to millions of devices)

g.data-narration: Scale and heterogeneity require a unified management platform to handle diverse protocols like WiFi, Zigbee, and Bluetooth
g.text: Thousands to millions of diverse devices: different hardware, protocols (WiFi, Bluetooth, Zigbee, LoRaWAN),
and firmware versions. Without unified management, it becomes unmanageable chaos.

g.data-narration: Remote deployment requires management capabilities for monitoring, configuration, and FOTA updates without physical access
g.text: Devices deployed in remote, inaccessible locations (bridges, underground, inside machines).
Physical access is difficult and expensive. Management enables:

g.data-narration: IoT generates massive data requiring edge processing and cloud analytics to transform raw data into actionable insights
g.text: Millions of devices generate torrents of raw data. Value lies in insights, not just data collection.

g.data-narration: IoT security requires provisioning, monitoring, policy enforcement, and rapid patching across all devices
g.text: IoT devices are vulnerable entry points. A single unmanaged device can compromise an entire network.

g.data-narration: Lifecycle management covers provisioning, operation, and secure decommissioning to prevent ghost devices
g.text: Management covers the entire device journey, from onboarding to secure decommissioning.

g.data-narration: Summary covers five key areas: scale, remote operations, data insights security and full lifecycle management
IoT systems management transforms chaotic connected devices into reliable, efficient, and secure operational systems
g.text: IoT Systems Management is NOT optional. It is a fundamental necessity for any large-scale deployment.
It transforms chaotic connected things into reliable, efficient, secure, and valuable operational systems.
